hvtiPlotR 1.1.0

hvtiPlotR: Publication-Quality Graphics for Clinical Manuscripts

Source: R/help.R
hvtiPlotR-package.Rd

hvtiPlotR is an R port of the plot.sas macro suite used by the Clinical Investigations Statistics group within the Heart & Vascular Institute at the Cleveland Clinic. It produces publication-quality graphics that conform to HVI manuscript and presentation standards using ggplot2::ggplot2 and the officer::officer package.

All plot functions return bare ggplot2::ggplot() objects (or lists of them) so callers can apply additional ggplot2 layers, scales, and themes without restriction.

Themes

  • hvti_theme(): Unified theme dispatcher ("manuscript", "ppt", "dark_ppt", "poster").

  • theme_man() / theme_manuscript: Theme for manuscript figures.

  • theme_ppt(): Theme for PowerPoint presentation figures.

  • theme_dark_ppt(): Dark theme for PowerPoint presentations.

  • theme_poster(): Theme for poster figures.

Output helpers

  • save_ppt(): Save ggplot objects to a PowerPoint presentation via the officer::officer package.

  • makeFootnote(): Add footnotes to graphics.

Plot functions

  • hvti_plot(): Single entry-point dispatcher for all hvtiPlotR plot types ("mirror_histogram", "stacked_histogram", "covariate_balance").

  • mirror_histogram(): Side-by-side propensity-score histograms (binary-match or IPTW-weighted mode). Ports plot.sas mirror histogram output.

  • stacked_histogram(): Stacked (or filled) histogram of a numeric variable faceted by a grouping factor.

  • covariate_balance(): Dot-plot of standardised mean differences before and after propensity-score matching or weighting.

  • goodness_followup(): Goodness-of-follow-up scatter plot showing actual vs. potential follow-up per operation year. Optionally includes a non-fatal-event panel.

  • survival_curve(): Kaplan-Meier or Nelson-Aalen survival analysis returning up to five plot types (survival, cumulative hazard, hazard, log-log, life/RMST) plus risk and report tables. Ports the SAS %kaplan and %nelsont macros from tp.ac.dead.sas.

Sample-data generators

Each plot function ships with a companion generator for use in examples and tests:

  • sample_mirror_histogram_data(): Simulates propensity scores via a logistic model with greedy 1:1 caliper matching and optional IPTW weights.

  • sample_stacked_histogram_data(): Simulates year-by-category count data.

  • sample_covariate_balance_data(): Simulates patient-level covariates with a logistic propensity model and caliper matching, returning standardised mean differences before and after matching.

  • sample_goodness_followup_data(): Simulates an operative cohort with operation dates, follow-up times, death, and non-fatal events.

  • sample_survival_data(): Simulates exponential survival times with administrative censoring and optional treatment strata.

References

Wickham, H. ggplot2: Elegant Graphics for Data Analysis. Springer, 2009.

On this page

John Ehrlinger
